example.es: A mí también me gusta comer pho.
example.en: I also like to eat pho.

notes:
cũng means also / too. You use it to say something is the same or similar to another thing.

It usually goes after the subject and before the main verb or adjective.  

It can link ideas across sentences, showing agreement or similarity. It works with verbs and adjectives.

Very close to English “also”; sometimes like “too” at the end of a sentence in English, but in Vietnamese it stays before the verb.
exampleSentences: Em cũng thích cà phê đen.

translation.en: I also like to eat pho.

sentenceBreakdowns: Em (I) + cũng (also) + thích (like) + cà phê (coffee) + đen (black)
Chị (she) + cũng (also) + xem (watch) + phim hài (comedy movie)
notes_md:
**cũng** means **also / too**. You use it to say something is the same or similar to another thing.

It usually goes **after the subject and before the main verb or adjective**.  

It can link ideas across sentences, showing agreement or similarity. It works with verbs and adjectives.

Very close to English **“also”**; sometimes like **“too”** at the end of a sentence in English, but in Vietnamese it stays before the verb.
